title: Netskope Admin Audit Trail
spl:
index=casb sourcetype="netskope:audit" earliest=-30d
| stats count earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last values(operation) as ops by admin_user, object_type
| sort -last
description: Administrative changes to Netskope policies, steering configs, and tenant settings have global impact. Audit trail ensures accountability and change correlation.
value: Security governance teams audit all Netskope administrative changes — policy modifications, protection disables, admin account additions — flagging out-of-hours changes and high-impact deletions that can create instant organization-wide security gaps.
implementation: Alert on after-hours policy changes or bulk rule modifications. Correlate admin changes with enforcement anomalies in UC-17.3.37. Require change tickets for policy modifications.
visualization: Table (recent changes), Timeline (admin activity), Bar chart (changes by admin).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Spikes from new SaaS rollouts, automated workflows, sanctioned bulk uploads, or M&A integration.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Netskope admin audit events in `index=netskope` with `sourcetype=netskope:audit` or from the Netskope REST API audit endpoint. Key fields: `admin_user`, `event` (Create/Update/Delete/Login), `object_type` (Policy/Profile/Publisher/Configuration), `object_name`, `details`, `timestamp`.
• Netskope admin changes can have immediate, organization-wide impact. A misconfigured DLP policy, disabled threat protection, or removed CASB rule can create an instant security gap.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify admin audit events:
```spl
index=netskope sourcetype="netskope:audit" earliest=-30d
| stats count by event, object_type
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Admin change log:
```spl
index=netskope sourcetype="netskope:audit" earliest=-7d
| eval change_hour=strftime(_time, "%H")
| eval out_of_hours=if(change_hour<"08" OR change_hour>"18", "YES", "no")
| table _time, admin_user, event, object_type, object_name, details, out_of_hours
| sort - _time
```

Understanding this SPL: Lists all admin changes with out-of-hours flagging. Critical changes: deleting a real-time protection policy, disabling threat protection, modifying DLP rules, or adding admin users. Out-of-hours changes bypass the normal review process and are higher risk.

High-impact changes:
```spl
index=netskope sourcetype="netskope:audit" earliest=-7d
| where event=="Delete" OR match(object_type, "(?i)policy|protection|dlp")
| stats count by admin_user, event, object_type
| sort - count
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Make a test change in Netskope Admin. Verify the audit event.
(b) In Netskope: Settings > Administration > Audit Log. Compare.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Admin changes per day. Row 2 — High-impact changes. Row 3 — Out-of-hours changes.
Alert: Immediate on policy deletion or threat protection disable.

Runbook:
1. **Policy deletion** — Verify with admin. Roll back if unintended.
2. **Threat protection disabled** — Critical. Re-enable immediately.
3. **New admin added** — Verify authorization with security team.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No audit events** — Audit logging may require a separate API endpoint. Check Netskope REST API v2.
• **`details` empty** — Some admin actions don't include change details in the audit.
grandmaExplanation: Every change an administrator makes to our cloud security settings is recorded. We pay special attention to dangerous changes like disabling security features or deleting rules, especially when they happen outside normal hours, because one wrong change can leave the entire organization unprotected.
